old_claim.note: Steel Production provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Stainless steel is a chromium-bearing steel alloy; the scoped technology requires prior controlled steel production before chromium alloying can yield rustless/stainless steel.

source_shape.source_locator: British Stainless Steel Association article identifies Brearley's 13 August 1913 steel as 12.8% chromium and 0.24% carbon and frames it as stainless/rustless steel.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The scoped stainless-steel technology is a chromium-bearing steel alloy, so steel production is part of the material's defining architecture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a required edge because the dependent is not just corrosion resistance generally; it is stainless steel, an alloyed form of steel.

ontology_before: The graph treated steel production as merely enabling even though stainless steel is itself a steel alloy.
ontology_after: The graph treats steel production as a hard prerequisite for the scoped chromium steel alloy.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: steel_production -> stainless_steel edge is now required.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped away from steel alloys to corrosion-resistant alloys generally.
A separate chromium-steel precursor node replaces direct steel_production as the hard prerequisite.
short_reason: Stainless steel is a steel alloy, so steel production is required for the scoped node.
